DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT INSTABASE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAMINATION.DEFENDANT INSTABASE, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF, SAMATHA LUCAS IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:(1) PLAINTIFF WILL SUBMIT TO A MENTAL EXAMINATION BEFORE MARK A. KALISH, M.D. ON A DATE TO BE AGREED UPON BETWEEN THE PARTIES.(2) THE SCOPE OF THE EXAMINATION SHALL BE AS SET FORTH IN DR. KALISH'S DECLARATION AND SHALL INCLUDE THE MINNESOTA MULTIPHASIC PERSONALITY INVENTORY 2 AS WELL AS A PSYCHIATRIC INTERVIEW. THIS INTERVIEW SHALL NOT ENTAIL ANY INQUIRY INTO PLAINTIFF'S SEXUAL HISTORY OTHER THAN SUCH INQUIRY AS IT RELATES TO DEFENDANT CLARK.(3) UNLESS PLAINTIFF CONSENTS TO AN IN-PERSON INTERVIEW, THE EXAMINATION MAY BE CONDUCTED REMOTELY; HOWEVER, NO PERSON WILL BE PERMITTED TO BE IN THE ROOM WITH PLAINTIFF DURING THE INTERVIEW. AN AUDIO RECORDING OF THE EXAMINTION WILL BE MADE AND RETAINED BY DR. KALISH.(4) THE LENGTH OF THE EXAMINATION SHALL NOT EXCEED FIVE HOURS.(5) WITHIN THIRTY(30) DAYS FOLLOWING COMPLETION OF THE EXAMINATION, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL BE PROVIDED WITH A COPY OF DR. KALISH'S REPORT. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER EMAILED BY COUNSEL DIRECTLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM RENNE WHICH MIRRORS THE BODY OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND APPROVED AS TO FORM BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar May-31-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination
Off Calendar May-31-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling to the unopposed motion as follows:

DEFENDANT INSTABASE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination.

DEFENDANT INSTABASE, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF, SAMATHA LUCAS IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:

(1) PLAINTIFF WILL SUBMIT TO A MENTAL EXAMINATION BEFORE MARK A. KALISH, M.D. ON A DATE TO BE AGREED UPON BETWEEN THE PARTIES.

(2) THE SCOPE OF THE EXAMINATION SHALL BE AS SET FORTH IN DR. KALISH'S DECLARATION AND SHALL INCLUDE THE MINNESOTA MULTIPHASIC PERSONALITY INVENTORY 2 AS WELL AS A PSYCHIATRIC INTERVIEW. THIS INTERVIEW SHALL NOT ENTAIL ANY INQUIRY INTO PLAINTIFF'S SEXUAL HISTORY OTHER THAN SUCH INQUIRY AS IT RELATES TO DEFENDANT CLARK.

(3) UNLESS PLAINTIFF CONSENTS TO AN IN-PERSON INTERVIEW, THE EXAMINATION MAY BE CONDUCTED REMOTELY; HOWEVER, NO PERSON WILL BE PERMITTED TO BE IN THE ROOM WITH PLAINTIFF DURING THE INTERVIEW. AN AUDIO RECORDING OF THE EXAMINTION WILL BE MADE AND RETAINED BY DR. KALISH.

(4) THE LENGTH OF THE EXAMINATION SHALL NOT EXCEED FIVE HOURS.

(5) WITHIN THIRTY(30) DAYS FOLLOWING COMPLETION OF THE EXAMINATION, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL BE PROVIDED WITH A COPY OF DR. KALISH'S REPORT.

The Court will sign the proposed order emailed by Counsel directly to Judge Pro Tem Renne which mirrors the body of the tentative ruling and approved as to form by Plaintiff's Counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
